data.title: TransThera Sciences Widens Annual Loss Amid Continued R&D Spend
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc. ( (HK:2617) ).

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc., a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical developer, reported no revenue for 2025 as it continued to focus on its research pipeline. The company’s cost base is dominated by research and development and administrative expenses, underscoring its status as an R&D-driven business without marketed products.

For the year ended 31 December 2025, the group posted a net loss of RMB295.96 million, widening from RMB274.61 million a year earlier, driven mainly by sustained high R&D spending and higher administrative costs. Other income and gains declined year-on-year, while basic and diluted loss per share increased to RMB0.76 from RMB0.72, highlighting ongoing cash burn and underlining execution and funding risks typical for pre-commercial biotech issuers.

More about TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed biopharmaceutical company focused on drug discovery and development. The group operates as a pre-revenue enterprise, with its activities centered on research and development rather than commercial sales, reflecting its positioning in the early- to mid-stage innovative therapeutics segment.

Many Idea Cloud Holdings Limited ( (HK:6696) ) just unveiled an update.

Many Idea Cloud Holdings Limited reported the results of its extraordinary general meeting held in Hong Kong on 31 March 2026, where shareholders considered resolutions related to a new share award scheme. All directors except two attended in person or electronically, with the full issued share capital of 672 million shares eligible to vote and no shareholders required to abstain under listing rules.

Shareholders overwhelmingly approved both the adoption of the company’s share award scheme and the associated service provider sublimit, with 100% of the 314,540,077 votes cast in favor and none against. data.title: Winfair Investment Reshapes Board With INED Resignation and New Appointment
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Winfair Investment Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0287) ) has shared an announcement.

Winfair Investment Company Limited has announced the resignation of independent non-executive director (INED) Ms. Chan Suit Fei, Esther, effective 31 March 2026, in order to comply with Hong Kong listing tenure requirements and to avoid potential conflicts of interest linked to a possible leasing arrangement involving her personal business and connected persons. Her departure also triggers changes in committee roles, as she steps down from the chairmanship and membership of the Nomination Committee and relinquishes her positions on the Audit, Remuneration and Corporate Governance Committees, with the board noting she has no disagreements or issues requiring shareholder attention.

On the same date, the company has appointed Mr. Yip Sing as an INED and member of the Audit, Remuneration, Nomination and Corporate Governance Committees, strengthening the board’s financial expertise with his more than 15 years of experience in accounting, auditing, internal control and financial consultancy. In related governance adjustments, non-executive director Ms. Ng Kwok Fun joins the Nomination Committee and existing INED Dr. Ng Chi Yeung, Simon becomes its chairman, signaling a refreshed committee structure intended to maintain regulatory compliance and reinforce independent oversight for shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0287) stock is a Hold with a HK$4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Winfair Investment Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:0287 Stock Forecast page.

More about Winfair Investment Co., Ltd.

The company is governed by a board that includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, supported by key committees overseeing audit, remuneration, nomination and corporate governance matters.

Analogue Holdings Limited has announced that non-executive director Ms. Or Siu Ching, Rerina will resign from the board with effect from 1 April 2026 upon the expiry of her term of appointment, citing her retirement plan and confirming there is no disagreement with the board or the company. Silkwave Inc has amended and restated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee with effect from 31 March 2026, clarifying its mandate to review board composition and recommend procedures and criteria for appointing directors. Poly Property Services Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6049) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Poly Property Services Co., Ltd. reported steady growth for 2025, with revenue rising 4.8% to RMB17.13 billion, driven mainly by a 12.6% increase in property management services, while value-added services to non-property owners and community value-added services both declined. An announcement from Sihuan Pharmaceutical Holdings Group ( (HK:0460) ) is now available.

Sihuan Pharmaceutical reported a sharp rebound in 2025, with total revenue rising 37.7% year on year to RMB2.62 billion and group gross profit climbing 45.1% to RMB1.80 billion, lifting gross margin to 68.8%. The performance marks a shift in the company’s revenue mix, as higher-margin medical aesthetics became its largest business, exceeding half of total sales for the first time.

The medical aesthetics segment nearly doubled revenue to RMB1.49 billion, driven by strong uptake of core product Letybo, broader channel coverage to more than 8,000 institutions, and rapid growth from newly approved PLLA and PCL fillers and a niche facial revitalizer. Innovative and other medicines more than tripled sales on the back of diabetes products and initial commercialization at Xuanzhu Biopharm, while the generic drug segment declined 13%, with cost controls helping offset profit pressure and reinforcing the strategic pivot toward premium, high-growth aesthetics and innovative therapies.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

LifeTech Scientific Corporation ( (HK:1302) ) has provided an update.

LifeTech Scientific Corporation, a Hong Kong-listed medical device maker specializing in cardiovascular and endovascular products such as stent grafts, reported modest top-line growth for 2025 driven primarily by higher stent graft sales. The group maintains a strong gross margin profile, reflecting the continued strength of its core implantable device franchise in China.

For the year ended 31 December 2025, revenue rose 5.1% to RMB1,369.8 million and gross profit increased 3.8% to RMB1,019.1 million, but reported net profit fell 33.3% to RMB112.4 million as higher share-based payment expenses, increased income tax charges and fair-value losses on financial liabilities weighed on earnings. Excluding non-recurring items, underlying net profit climbed 24.9% to RMB353.4 million, yet earnings per share declined to RMB3.4 basic and the board again opted not to declare a final dividend, signaling a continued focus on reinvestment and cost of incentive schemes over near-term shareholder payouts.

SIIC Environment Holdings ( (SG:BHK) ) has issued an update.

SIIC Environment Holdings’ subsidiary Longjiang Environmental Protection Group has agreed to acquire 100% equity interests in Anshan-based Qingchang Water Services and Qinglang Water Services from Qingdao Qing’an Technology Investment for RMB 270 million. The deal will make both water service operators wholly owned subsidiaries, with their results consolidated into the group’s financial statements.

The consideration was determined using income-based valuations prepared by an independent appraisal firm, applying a weighted average cost of capital model to the target companies’ equity. Directors view the pricing as fair and commercially reasonable, signaling a strategic move to strengthen the group’s water services footprint and potentially enhance earnings through greater scale in China’s municipal water sector.

More about SIIC Environment Holdings

SIIC Environment Holdings, through its controlling subsidiary Longjiang Environmental Protection Group, operates in the environmental protection and water services industry in China. The group focuses on water treatment and related municipal services, expanding its portfolio of wastewater and water utility assets across regional markets, particularly via acquisitions that deepen its presence in key Chinese cities.

The latest announcement is out from The United Laboratories International Holdings ( (HK:3933) ).

The United Laboratories International Holdings has issued a clarification to its previously published annual results announcement for the year ended 31 December 2025, correcting an error in the description of its dividend arrangements. The company stated that references to a “special dividend” in the closure of register of members section should be removed, confirming that only a final dividend is applicable.

The clarification specifies the record date and share transfer cut-off for shareholders to qualify for the final dividend, with the register of members to be closed on 9 and 10 July 2026 and transfers to be lodged by 4:30 p.m. on 8 July 2026. The board emphasized that aside from this wording correction, all other information in the original results announcement remains unchanged, indicating no alteration to the company’s previously disclosed financial outcomes or dividend policy.

data.description:

Poly Property Services Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6049) ) has shared an update.

Poly Property Services has completed the nomination process for its fourth board session, proposing a slate that includes non-executive, executive, and independent non-executive directors for shareholder approval at the 2025 annual general meeting. If approved, the new board will serve a three-year term, with director service contracts renewed and remuneration set in line with company rules.

To ensure operational continuity, the term of the current third board session, originally expiring on 16 May 2026, will be extended until the new board is elected at the annual meeting. The mandates of board committees and senior management will also be prolonged, and the company states that the delayed board election will not disrupt normal operations, with further details to be provided in the forthcoming AGM circular.
